summary: All 6 hivefiver components fail the user's project+global scope requirements. Tools are structurally wired but substantively placeholder. Zero global path resolution. Zero .opencode/ content detection. Zero archive-vs-merge capability. Config groups read schema defaults, never disk state.

hm_init.key_issue: Only checks .hivemind/ existence; never reads .opencode/ or ~/.config/opencode/

hm_doctor.key_issue: Checks .opencode/ dir existence but never reads content; no global path scanning

hm_setting.key_issue: getConfigGroup() returns schema defaults via UserPreferences.parse({}), never reads opencode.json. _projectRoot param unused.

config_groups.key_issue: Pure schema defaults. No I/O. getConfigGroup() calls UserPreferences.parse({}) always.

paths.key_issue: getEffectivePaths() only returns .hivemind/ subdirs. No getGlobalPaths(). Zero grep matches for global path patterns.

blocked_routes: Cannot verify 'respects existing configs' — no actual read logic exists to test
Cannot verify 'archive vs merge' — no such options exist in any tool
recommended_next_action: Implement global path resolution layer in paths.ts, then update each hivefiver tool to detect and respect both project and global config levels before proposing changes
